name: Flower Tart - Country Living Holidays
recipeIngredient: Sugared Flowers, Petals, or Leaves (separate recipe)
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: This elegant tart with sugared-flower topping can be made with an almond filling for a spectacular holiday dessert or without the filling for a table or sideboard holiday display that will last several days.
1. Several hours or day before, prepare Sugared Flowers, Petals, or Leaves. At least 45 minutes before, prepare Pastry.
2. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). On a floured surface, roll out the pastry to an 11-inch round. Fit the pastry into a 9-inch fluted tart pan with removable bottom. Line the pastry shell with aluminum foil and fill with pie weights or beans. Bake for 10 minutes. Remove the foil and weights; bake the shell for 10 more minutes. Set aside. If making for display, cool and fill with Sugared Flowers, Petals, or Leaves at this point.
3. In a food processor fitted with a chopping blade, grind 1 cup almonds with the sugar until very fine.
4. In a large bowl, beat the butter and almond-sugar mixture with an electric mixer on medium speed until combined. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in flour and almond extract just until combined and stiff batter forms.
5. Sprinkle the remaining 1/2 cup almonds over the bottom of the tart shell. Gently pour the batter over the almonds.
6. Bake the tart for 20 minutes or until golden. Allow to cool completely on a wire rack. Remove the tart from the pan; place on a serving platter and top with Sugared Flowers, Petals, or Leaves. Cut into 10 wedges to serve. Pastry: In a medium-sized bowl, combine 1 1/4 cups unsifted all-purpose flour and 1/4 teaspoon salt. With a pastry blender or 2 knives, cut in 6 tablespoons butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle 2 to 3 tablespoons cold water, 1 tablespoon at a time, over the flour mixture and mix lightly with a fork until the pastry is moist enough to press into a ball. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
